title: Bolt Torque Calculator
description: Industrial-grade bolt torque calculation tool based on ISO 898, VDI 2230, and ASME PCC-1 standards. Computes optimal torque, preload, and joint integrity metrics with loss-driver analysis.

inputs.label: Bolt Grade / Property Class
inputs.businessContext: Defines yield and tensile strength per ISO 898-1.

inputs.label: Nominal Bolt Diameter (d)
inputs.businessContext: Major diameter of the bolt thread.

inputs.label: Thread Pitch (P)
inputs.businessContext: Distance between thread crests. Coarse or fine.

inputs.label: Friction Coefficient (Thread)
inputs.businessContext: µ_th: affects torque-preload relationship. Typical range 0.08-0.20 for lubricated steel.

inputs.label: Friction Coefficient (Head/Nut face)
inputs.businessContext: µ_h: underhead or nut face friction. Higher values reduce preload efficiency.

inputs.label: Target Preload (% of Yield)
inputs.businessContext: Typical assembly preload target. ISO 898 recommends 70-80% for static joints.

inputs.label: Lubrication Condition
inputs.businessContext: Affects friction coefficients and torque scatter.

inputs.label: Operating Temperature
inputs.businessContext: High temperature reduces yield strength and alters friction.

inputs.label: Use Washers (Under Head & Nut)
inputs.businessContext: Washers reduce embedment and distribute load.

rules.message: Thread friction should typically be less than or equal to head friction for consistent torque-preload behavior.
Preload above 85% of yield may cause bolt yielding or fracture. Verify joint design.
Very coarse thread relative to diameter may reduce thread stripping strength.

thresholds.message: Torque exceeds 500 N·m. Consider hydraulic tensioning or multi-pass tightening.
Preload efficiency below 40%. High friction losses. Review lubrication and surface finish.
Clamp load insufficient for joint sealing or slip resistance.

formulas.name: Tensile Stress Area (A_s)
formulas.description: Effective cross-sectional area of bolt per ISO 898.

formulas.name: Yield Strength (σ_y)
formulas.description: Minimum yield strength based on property class.

formulas.name: Target Preload (F_p)
formulas.description: Desired bolt preload as percentage of yield load.

formulas.name: Torque Coefficient (K)
formulas.description: Nut factor based on friction coefficients and geometry.
formulas.notes: d_head approximated as 1.5*d for hex bolts.

formulas.name: Calculated Torque (T)
formulas.description: Torque required to achieve target preload.

formulas.name: Preload Efficiency (η)
formulas.description: Ratio of preload to input torque energy.

formulas.name: Clamp Load (F_c)
formulas.description: Actual clamp force after considering joint stiffness and embedment.
formulas.notes: embedment_loss_factor = 0.05 for steel-steel with washers, 0.10 without washers.

components.label: Head/Nut Face Friction Torque
components.description: Torque consumed by underhead or nut face friction.

components.label: Preload (Stretch) Torque
components.description: Torque converted into bolt stretch (useful work).

suggestedActions.description: Use calibrated torque wrench with accuracy ±3% or better.
Apply molybdenum disulfide paste to threads and underhead to reduce friction scatter.
Perform torque-tension verification test on 5% of bolts per batch.
For critical joints, consider hydraulic tensioning instead of torque control.
Re-torque after 24 hours to compensate for embedment loss.
